products.bookDescription: A truly innovative puzzle that incorporates murder mysteries, environmental storytelling, sudoku-like logic ... and now, time travel! Place the suspects, reveal the killer! These visually appealing, addictive puzzles drop solvers into immersive mini murder mysteries, combining features of classic Japanese-style logic puzzles like sudoku with the storytelling of classic grid logic problems. Each Murdoku grid is its own unique crime scene in which every detail is important: rooms, furniture, plants, animals, and most importantly, the suspects. Using the clues, you must find each suspect's location to reveal the killer. This time, though, there's a twist: you've stumbled upon a time portal that's brought you to the past. You'll visit the Stone Age, medieval times, the Old West, ancient Egypt, and the Golden Age of Piracy on your way back to your own time. Each era has its own flavor, but one thing is always true: there are murders to solve. Are you up for the challenge?

products.bookDescription: NATIONAL BESTSELLER • The Worldwide Puzzle Phenomenon • "Clever, satisfying and utterly addictive." ―Janice Hallett, bestselling author of The Appeal G. T. Karber, the creator of the popular online daily mystery game Murdle, presents the next collection of 100 original murder mystery logic puzzles―a perfect gift for armchair detectives and puzzlers to hone their minds and solve a series of crimes.Who committed the ghastly deed?What weapon was used to dispatch the victim?Where did the calculated demise occur? Deductive Logico is back on the scene in Murdle: Volume 2. Based on the sensational hit online game, these clever and hilarious mini-mystery puzzles will challenge you to find whodunit, how, where, and why. Unearth the dark secrets of the Violet Isles, solve the riddles of an ancient and mysterious scholar, and explore the haunted lands of Drakonia. . . if you dare.Packed with pictures, codes, and maps, and brimming with wit and intrigue, Murdle: Volume 2 is a thrilling casebook for the armchair detective in each of us.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Challenging and Addictive Logic Puzzle Book
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: If you love murder-mystery logic puzzles, Murdle: Volume 2 delivers another fun batch of 100 cases to crack using deduction, clues, and grid logic. The puzzles flow from easy to truly tricky, and they’re great for anyone who enjoys teasing their brain while slowly unraveling the mystery of who did it, how, and where. I appreciated the variety and the way the difficulty ramps up as you go through the book.The layout with suspects, weapons, locations, and clever clues makes you think — but it’s not overly frustrating if you enjoy that kind of challenge. I found myself coming back to it during breaks, road trips, or quiet evenings.Some puzzles might feel repetitive if you’re used to logic books, and a few can be head-scratching (maybe too much so) if you’re new to this style — but that’s part of the appeal for puzzle fans.Overall, if you enjoyed the first Murdle book or like deductive reasoning games, this is a solid sequel that keeps your mind engaged. Worth picking up!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The good news is that you get your 1,000 puzzles.Was purchased for my wife and here is what we discussed:I don't like this...pauseWhy?There isn't any room for my numbers.What?There are so many puzzles stuffed on one page there isn't room for me to write in the margins.Ok, what else?There isn't an answer page.You mean diagrams with the finished puzzles ?Yes.Anything else?Since there are so many on a page they are hard to see and work.OK, Should I return it?Yes...If you're a Sudoku fan this probably makes sense.AMAZON is stunning in dealing with customer service and handled this to our satisfaction.From my side there are large pages which may or may not be useable, I'd like to see far fewer per page and more pages.When watching her solve Sudokus I sometimes observe her checking the back for finished puzzle.This may or may not be important to you.Returned to the original ad and looked around.Found a READ SAMPLE button which actually does show a actual page.Had we used this initially another book would have been selected.So I'm partially to blame.If you want anything over the 1,000 (1000+ advertised) you have to go online and download/print them.My choices are in one star increments so I gave four.Given other options it would be 4.5

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Good Sudoku book for beginners.  The "easy" puzzles have been so absurdly simple (even worked "the hard way") that they are fairly worthless for a seasoned solver.  "The hard way" for me: count the ones and nines, whichever there a fewer of, start by working that number until all 9 are filled in, without writing in any other numbers.  If I start with ones, then I fill in the twos next, then threes, etcetera, until all the remaining blanks are the nines.  This makes a reasonably easy puzzle to be more like a medium (or even a hard puzzle sometimes).

products.bookDescription: Place the suspects, reveal the killer! These visually appealing, addictive puzzles drop solvers into immersive mini murder mysteries, combining the elegant simplicity of games like sudoku with the storytelling of classic logic problems. But Murdoku introduces its own unique element: full-color visual representations of the characters' environments, with each element playing into the investigation: rooms, furniture, plants, animals, and sometimes additional rules that add an extra twist. You’ll explore 80 crime scenes including a bakery, a casino, a chess tournament, a farm, an opera, … the variety is endless. The puzzles start out easy and gradually increase in difficulty to expert level. Do you have what it takes to become a master detective?

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Beyond Enjoyable and Love the Challenge
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: As a lifelong puzzle enthusiast, I genuinely consider these to be among the most captivating puzzles I have ever encountered. They flawlessly merge the simple yet sophisticated structure of classic grid puzzles like Sudoku with intricate themes and compelling storylines that draw me in. Whether we're unraveling the mystery of a murder at an elegant opera house or deducing who killed a hockey referee, each puzzle presents a unique deduction challenge and injects a sense of *murder* intrigue that completely engrosses me. My husband and I enjoy working on them together during our weekend morning breakfast, finding them to be a perfect way to start the day. The grid layout not only enhances their visual appeal but also adds to their complexity, making them highly challenging and immensely enjoyable, especially as we piece together clues and try to solve them before finishing our meal.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I am an avid fan of both sudoku and logic puzzles and was excited to come across this Murdoku book. It is challenging but not overwhelming if you read the instructions and start at the beginning. Sometimes, though, I'd want to know the why of a solution statement. For example: A must be located in C4R8 (Column 4, Row 8). Why must A be located in C4R8? For me, sometimes I couldn't immediately understand the why. When I found myself in that position, I went back to the puzzle and thought through it more and often had that aha! moment.The order of the puzzles keeps building you up as you move forward. I found myself jumping ahead for more of a challenge before I was ready, but the detailed solutions in the back helped me get my thinking on track and I went back to previous difficulties. I followed the logic statements, positions, and relationships pretty well, but what took the most practice for me was to also get used to the sudoku aspect - only one person per column and row. Some things were obvious once I reviewed with sudoku in mind. Just something I needed to get used to.The puzzles and artwork are very visually appealing. You can tell a lot of of thought and detail went into both the puzzles and the presentation. I would buy every version of this book if they continued with the same standards.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Absolutely brilliant fun, very highly recommended!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Absolutely brilliant fun! Far more engaging than Sudoku or Murdle. Each puzzle is a self-contained murder mystery in a different setting—each a set of furnished rooms with layout, suspects and clues. The colour visual/graphical element is captivating, the varying themes (and subtitles) clever and entertaining (e.g., “8. Backyard Jacuzzi. Looks like someone’s temper boiled over”). The increasing difficulty keeps drawing one back to solve the next puzzle as well. The early warm-up puzzles can be solved quickly enough; more challenging ones take upwards of 15-20 minutes, probably even longer, later on—we are only 1/3 into our book(s) now. (Spouse & I each have our own copies and solve/semi-compete simultaneously across the room from each other, though neither of us writes in our books so I guess we will still have 2 pristine copies to cherish, at the end.) Very highly recommended! First discovered this via the UK Financial Times; the publisher Hachette has sampler pages for anyone to try—that’s what kept us going while awaiting the 28 Oct. release date. Bravo, Monsieur Garand!PS. For anyone trying the samplers, the instructions are available in the FT special puzzle page—best to do that first (google will find that). The key principles, though, are that suspects and victim (derived last) cannot share rows or columns, furniture squares except for chairs, beds, and rugs cannot be occupied, and “beside” or “next to” applies orthogonally only (not diagonally). The solutions at the back of the book do completely present the step-by-step logic for arriving at victim-murderer answers, too.PPS. (As of 28 November) There now appear to be sample pages on view even here on Amazon, rendering my postscript above perhaps unnecessary, though the FT & Hachette samples are actually puzzles. Most significant, however, is the brand-new Murdoku sampler website to feed any Murdoku addiction or post-Book 1 void on a semiweekly basis!

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Yes yes yes.... Worth every cent
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I'm absolutely hooked. This has helped me break my digital addiction. Would recommend

I've been following Murdoku since the beginning. As a lifetime puzzle lover, I don't mean it lightly when I say that these are my favourite puzzles of all time!!! These puzzles perfectly combine the simplicity and elegance of classic grid-based puzzles like Sudoku with creative locations and titillating plot. From solving a murder at an opera to deducing who killed the referee in the hockey game, each puzzle is a unique adventure of deduction and *murder* that always has me completedly pulled in. My fiancée and I love doing these together on our weekends mornings!There is so much replayability too because of the grid-based element. For this specific book, I personally used a transparent sheet and erasable markers, but I'm so happy to see that these are printed on high-quality paper that would endure my aggressive erasing. The puzzles are also excellently paced, starting out at a good difficulty for the majority of the book, increasing to some pretty tough ones near the end (would you believe #79 took me 4 hours?) Given that it took me a consistent few hours daily over more than a week to get through PLUS the replayability, definitely worth the price! I'll definitely be re-trying this book again in a few months! :)

aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
Over 150 developmentally appropriate activities are organized by subject and captivate a wide spectrum of learners. 
SPATIAL REASONING 
In the section that follows there’s so much to learn. You’ll be using your mind to rotate and turn. 
Kids will perform mental manipulations by visualizing the differences, movement and changes between shapes, spot patterns, and think about objects in three dimensions. 
MATH PUZZLES 
Work through the games until they’re done. It all adds up to lots of fun. 
Math meets fun as kids engage with number crossword, sudoku, animal math, optimization, problem-solving, constrained variables, and number patterns. 
LOGIC PROBLEMS 
Consider order step by step to figure out what happens next. 
Kids will love these problems featuring picture analogies, constraint handling, order sequencing, relative positioning, programming, deductive reasoning, and brain teasers. 
WORD GAMES 
Let's shift targets. Let's change gears. Words will be our focus here. 
Playing word games can improve problem-solving, memory, cognition, and creativity. This section contains scrambled words, crossword puzzles, word search, hidden words, ciphers, cryptography, and word puzzles. 
FOCUS ACTIVITIES 
Activities follow on pages beyond. You’ve got this. Keep at it. Keep moving on. 
Maze, seek and find, spot the difference, dot to dot, hidden objects, commonalities, and more! 
TWO-PLAYER GAMES 
Grab a parent or friend before you’ve adjourned. Put to use all the tools and logic you've learned. 
13 pencil and paper two-player games facilitate the direct application of acquired skills and provide the foundation for continued fun and growth after the book is finished.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I bought this workbook for my 5-year-old to keep his brain active over the summer holidays, and he was very keen to get started. I’d say 5 is about the youngest it would suit.The book includes a variety of puzzles—word searches, number challenges, logic activities, and problem-solving exercises. productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Brilliant book - what 6-10 years old kids need
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I am a retired math teacher. I have been disappointed with the lack of critical thinking materials my grandchildren use in school (SK - grade 2) This book (part of a larger series of 3-4 books) was a true saviour for us.  Not only it makes the kids THINK but my grandkids also ENJOYED doing the tasks/exercises/activities. (My grandson is 6, my granddaughter is 8). Mr Allbaugh (the author) has created (and maybe also collected?) a vast range of tasks - each more interesting than the other. The range of levels of difficulty is perfect: from the ones that would build confidence to ones that require patience and a few tries. Nothing tricky but nothing trivial either.If you care about developing your kids/grandkids critical thinking - do get this!PS: When a kid starts a new task I recommend an adult is there to make sure  the kid understands it correctly and starts it successfully.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription:
The media could not be loaded.
                
            
                
            
            
        
    
    








  
  
    I bought this puzzle book as a Christmas gift for my dad who wants to maintain his cognition. He used to do word game apps on his phone, so I figured a physical book would lower screen time. When we opened the book for Christmas, the whole family was excited to do crossword puzzles together! It is a good bonding experience for the holidays. The book includes enriching puzzles ranging from easy, to medium, to difficult. There is information about how to solve each puzzle type and the cognitive benefits of doing puzzles. I believe that everyone should try to incorporate brain games in their daily routine, however, this book seems to be advanced. My dad cannot do more than word searches while the rest of the family struggles with some crossword questions. The logic puzzles are particularly my personal favorite. Bottom line: the book is perfect if you are an adult who seeks a challenge. It seems too difficult for my mid 50-year-old parents and late 20 year-old siblings.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This is a great bargain.  I've gone full-circle on Sudoku puzzles.  Worked my way from easy up to expert and even the super-sudoku puzzles.  Now I've gone back to the beginning working on speed solving puzzles.  This assortment is great for chewing through a lot of puzzles quickly.  Here's a hint for these books...What I do is tear the cover off first.  Then I speed-solve the puzzle on the right page, and after this I tear the page off and flip it over to solve the left (facing) page of the next puzzle.  Once all the puzzles are solved on the front and back of each sheet I just discard the page.  This way the top page is always a fresh puzzle and you're not hassling with all the old previous puzzle pages, AND you never have to solve a puzzle on the facing page with nothing to support the puzzle.  You periodically have to clean the binding gum off the spine to make tearing the pages off neater (without ripping) to make this work, but a pair of scissors handles this in a few seconds.  Great bargain for the Sudoku fanatics out there like me.  Recommended!

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Perfect Murdle Fun, Perfectly Sized for Young Detectives!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: My 11-year-old son is a huge fan of the original Murdle puzzle books, enjoying both the challenge and the humor. When I saw Murdle Jr., I knew we had to give it a try, and I'm so glad we did! This book hits the sweet spot perfectly for its target audience.He dove right into "Curious Crimes for Curious Minds" and was instantly hooked. The puzzles retain the clever deductive reasoning core of the original Murdle, but are definitely geared appropriately for younger solvers. He found the difficulty level to be "just right" – challenging enough to make him think, but not so hard that he got frustrated. He loves the whole Murdle concept, and this junior version delivered that same fun experience.He was so engaged that he worked his way through the entire book in just a couple of days! For us, that's always a sign of a great puzzle book. It held his attention, provided a satisfying sense of accomplishment with each solved case, and kept him entertained screen-free.If you have a child around 10-12 years old who enjoys logic puzzles, mysteries, or is perhaps curious about the main Murdle books but finds them a bit daunting, Murdle Jr. is an absolutely fantastic introduction. It captures the spirit of the original while being perfectly accessible. Highly recommended!

products.bookDescription: Sudoku is a book for anyone who wants to spend their time actively. This is especially true for those who struggle to concentrate, those who want to unwind, and those who want to do something productive with their time.Too much free time, lack of activity, and concentration problems lead to stress, depression, and self-neglect. So, if you want to engage in something creative, calm, and unhurried that will force you to act, these number puzzles are perfect for you.Let this book spark your imagination and encourage logical thinking!Featuring 300 different puzzles with three levels of difficulty and variety, this book contains only one correct solution. It guarantees endless fun, strengthens concentration, and promotes learning for everyone.You can take it on a trip, on a hike, in the mountains, at work or simply solve it at home.By searching for solutions at various levels of difficulty, you foster creativity and nurture your development and manual skills. It relieves stress and helps you focus on the task at hand.This book contains:-Lots of puzzles: 300 Sudoku puzzles from easy to hard level to keep you entertained for hours-Large size & pages give You a lot of space to solve it-Tree levels of difficulty-Sudoku puzzles are fun and engaging to eliminate stress-Learn logical thinking-Instruction how to solve Sudoku step-by-step-Very nice mental break at the end or beginning of a dayIf you're new to Sudoku and don't know exactly how to solve a puzzle, the helpful instructions inside the book will help you get started. Later, as you gain experience and move on to a more challenging level, you'll choose your own method or technique through process of elimination. Solving will then become easier! If you're a Sudoku fan and enjoy puzzles, this book is perfect for you!Share the joy with a friend or family member.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Kakuro Instructions a Puzzle of Its Own
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This just arrived yesterday, so I haven't done much with it. The print quality is good. The puzzle type variance is pretty good also. I've never done a Kakuro puzzle before, so I actually had to read the instructions for it. This is the first problem I ran into. I found the Kakuro instructions confusing. As written, the instructions say to use the top-right number in the clue cell for filling in the white cells below the clue cell. As shown in the included photo, that doesn't work. At least not for the clue cells on the edges. It's the same for the bottom-left number in the clue cell, which, as written, should be used for filling in the white cells to the right of the clue cell. The clue instructions appear to be typed in reverse of how they should actually be used. Once I figured that out, I was able to start working on a Kakuro puzzle. Otherwise, it's a decent puzzle book.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Murdle: The School of Mystery is an awesome puzzle book that’s perfect for logic lovers and mystery fans. The 50 puzzles are cleverly designed — not too easy, not too hard — and each has that satisfying aha! moment when you finally crack the code.The school theme adds a fun twist, and the clues are clear but challenging enough to keep you thinking. I liked that the book gradually increases in difficulty, so you feel like you’re leveling up your skills as you go. The layouts and grids are easy to follow, and the writing keeps the tone light and engaging.Great for adults, teens, or kids who enjoy brain teasers — especially if you like puzzles that make you think, not just guess. Whether you’re working alone or doing them with friends/family, this book delivers hours of satisfying logic fun!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These books are fun & challenging but not so challenging that it's just frustrating. I do recommend buying the books in order. You can solve the puzzles without doing that, but in some of the story parts you will have greater context.Now, two things I don't like:1. i do not like the second set of puzzles in any of the books. they make no sense because you dont know where the murder took place so there is no way to spot the liar through contradicting evidence. it all looks fine so you have to look at the hint section. This is why i only gave 4 stars.2-and this has nothing to do with the book, it's just a notation. There is a reddit group that you are encouraged to join,  HOWEVER last time i was there, there was no moderator and people were incredibly rude and condescending about asking for help, even though that is the whole point. it was so bad i left the group & it actually turned me off from book two for quite some time.  i was actually made fun of and was told that anyone familiar with the english language would have gotten it. bear in mind these books are published in several languages & so there are people in the group who may not be overly familiar with english turn of phrase.  i just wanted to add this disclaimer.but overall i highly recommend these books for any mystery and logic puzzle lover

aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
The Ultimate Sudoku Challenge – 2026 Edition 
Smart Design. Trusted Quality. 300 Hand-Checked Sudoku Puzzles. 
At Mind & Logic Books, we set our own standard for 2026. This Sudoku puzzle book brings you 300 carefully curated puzzles across Easy, Medium, and Hard levels — all hand-checked to ensure a single clean solution. 
No broken grids. No duplicates. No confusion. 
Just pure, satisfying logic in a premium layout — made for thinkers who value quality over quantity. 
Level Up Your Sudoku Skills – One Puzzle at a Time 
Build focus, confidence, and consistency through daily Sudoku practice 
Whether you’re just getting started or ready for the next level, this book grows with you. 100 Easy, 100 Medium, and 100 Hard puzzles help build confidence and sharpen your logic at every stage. 
Optimized Layout for Maximum Clarity and Focus 
Enjoy large-print puzzles in a clean 8.5 x 11 format with full solutions 
We believe that clarity matters. This book uses a clean, easy-to-read layout with four puzzles per page and full solutions in the back. No clutter. No confusion. Just pure logic, comfortably presented. 
Sudoku That Feels Good to Solve 
Relax your mind. Train your focus. Enjoy every puzzle. 
Designed for a smooth solving experience — 
no clutter, no confusion, just clean logic and satisfaction.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: If you listen to any review, listen to mine. I’ve bought volume 1 and volume 2 of this brand at least 5 or 6 times and gotten them as presents multiple times.These are the only sudoku books I use. I’ve been doing sudoku multiple times a day for over a decade now. The size of the book, puzzles, and numbers is great (if you like bolder font, choose volume 2!). The difficulty is great, especially if you like hard ones! The easy ones are too easy for me but I do them anyway. Then the hard ones are great.The ONE issue: I KNOW these are cheaper books and that’s the reason I buy them. HOWEVER, if they just made the spiral spine more sturdy, this book would be perfect. You can see in my first picture that the spiral has come out a little bit in the back and it’s annoying cause the pages come out very easily at that point.Otherwise, dope product. Will continue to buy multiples at a time in case they ever stop making them. I’ve gotten two friends to purchase these cause they are great.
